argentina.title: Great Western Trail Argentina
argentina.subtitle: Great Western Trail: Argentina
argentina.description: Randomize neutral buildings, station masters, player buildings, and city maps for the Argentina map.

second-edition.title: Great Western Trail 2nd Edition
second-edition.subtitle: Great Western Trail: Second Edition
second-edition.description: Randomize buildings, station masters, and optional Simmental, Brahman, or Rails To The North content.

new-zealand.title: Great Western Trail New Zealand
new-zealand.subtitle: Great Western Trail: New Zealand
new-zealand.description: Randomize neutral buildings, harbor masters, player buildings, and deckbuilding modules for the New Zealand map.

argentina.app-title: Great Western Trail Argentina Setup Randomizer
argentina.further-steps-step1: From the Buenos Aires supply, draw 5 farmer tiles with an A on their back and place them one after the other face-up onto the game board. Where a tile is placed depends on its type:
argentina.further-steps-step1a: If it is green farmer tile, place it in the green area onto the empty space with the lowest number (1 is the lowest and 4 the highest).
argentina.further-steps-step1b: Follow the same procedure for the blue and orange area. A drawn tile is placed on the empty space with the lowest number (of the corresponding area).
argentina.further-steps-step-1c: If no empty space is left because all 4 spaces in this area are already occupied, return it to the bag and draw a new tile.
argentina.further-steps-step2: Start drawing tiles with a B on their back (which include only worker tiles) from the Buenos Aires supply. Put these worker tiles one after the other in the job market as follows:
argentina.further-steps-step21: Start at the top row and place the first drawn tile onto the space directly beneath the player count sign of your actual player count. Place the next tile onto the space to the right of that tile and continue like this rightwards.
argentina.further-steps-step22: After you have placed a tile onto the rightmost space of the top row, continue along the second row, where you place the next tile yet again onto the space in line with your player count sign. Then continue like this rightwards until you have placed a tile onto the rightmost space of the second row.
argentina.further-steps-step23: Afterwards continue with placing tiles in the third row (starting with the space in line with your player count sign) until you place a tile onto the space to the left of the job market token. This is the last tile. So depending on your player count, there are now 5, 8 or 11 worker tiles in the job market.
argentina.further-steps-step3: Now fill the 6 foresight spaces in Buenos Aires with tiles from the Buenos Aires supply:
argentina.further-steps-step31: Draw 2 tiles with an A on their back and place them face-up onto the two foresight spaces marked A.
argentina.further-steps-step32: Draw 2 tiles with a B on their back and place them face-up onto the two foresight spaces marked B.
argentina.further-steps-step33: Draw 2 tiles with a C on their back and place them face-up onto the two foresight spaces marked C.
argentina.further-steps-step4: Shuffle the 36 market cattle cards and place them as a face-down market stack beneath the lower right of the game board. Depending on your player count, draw a number of cattle cards from this stack:
argentina.further-steps-step4b: Furthermore place the 25 exhaustion cards as a face-up exhaustion stack to the right of the market stack.
argentina.further-steps-step5: Shuffle the 24 objective cards and place them as a face-down stack to the left of the game board. Then reveal 4 cards from this stack and display them face-up in a column below the stack.
argentina.further-steps-step6: Of the 18 ship tiles, lay out the 11 ships with the sun icon on the right side of the board. First place the ship showing a 0 next to Buenos Aires and then the 10 other ships in ascending order below it. Thus, the ship with the 18 is placed furthest down.
argentina.further-steps-step6a: This display is called loading zone.
argentina.further-steps-step6b: The remaining 7 ships (without a sun icon) are shuffled and placed as a face-down ship pile underneath.
argentina.further-steps-step7: Place the 3 city maps above the game board, with Le Havre to the left, Rotterdam in the middle, and Liverpool to the right. For your first game, turn all 3 maps to the a-side. Then, depending on your player count, cover a certain number of city centers with the cover tiles:
argentina.further-steps-step71: In a 2-player game: Place 1 cover tile fitting on each of the 3 city maps.
argentina.further-steps-step72: In a 3-player game: Place 1 cover tile fitting on Rotterdam. Return the two leftover to the box.
argentina.further-steps-step73: In a 4-player game: Return all cover tiles to the box.
argentina.further-steps-step81: 1st Player: 7 pesos, 4 cards and 1 exchange token
argentina.further-steps-step82: 2nd Player: 8 pesos, 5 cards and 1 exchange token
argentina.further-steps-step83: 3rd Player: 9 pesos, 6 cards and 1 exchange token
argentina.further-steps-step84: 4th Player: 10 pesos, 7 cards and 1 exchange token

second-edition.app-title: Great Western Trail 2nd Edition Setup Randomizer
second-edition.further-steps-step1: From the Kansas City supply, draw 7 tiles with a on their back and place them one after the other onto the game board. Where a tile is placed depends on its type:
second-edition.further-steps-step1a: If it is an outlaw tile (green or orange), place it in the outlaw section onto the empty space with the lowest number (1 to 9).
second-edition.further-steps-step1b: If it is a hazard tile, place it in its corresponding hazard section (flood, drought or rockfall) onto the empty space with the lowest number (1 to 4). If there is no empty space left, return it to the bag and draw a new tile.
second-edition.further-steps-step2: Start drawing tiles with a 2 on their back (which include only worker tiles). Put these worker tiles one after the other in the job market as follows:
second-edition.further-steps-step21: Start in the top row and place the first drawn tile onto the space directly beneath the player count sign of your actual player count.
second-edition.further-steps-step22: Place the next tile onto the space to the right of that tile and continue like this rightwards.
second-edition.further-steps-step23: After you have placed a tile onto the rightmost space of the top row, continue along the second row, where you place the next tile yet again onto the space in line with your player count sign.
second-edition.further-steps-step24: Then continue like this rightwards until you place a tile onto the space to the left of the job market token. This is the last tile. So depending on your player count, there are now 3, 5 or 7 worker tiles in the job market.
second-edition.further-steps-step3: Now fill the 6 foresight spaces in Kansas City with tiles from the Kansas City supply:
second-edition.further-steps-step31: Draw 2 tiles with a 1 on their back and place them face up onto the two foresight spaces marked 1
second-edition.further-steps-step32: Draw 2 tiles with a 2 on their back and place them face up onto the two foresight spaces marked 2
second-edition.further-steps-step33: Draw 2 tiles with a 3 on their back and place them face up onto the two foresight spaces marked 3.
second-edition.further-steps-step4: Shuffle the 36 market cattle cards and place them as a face-down market cattle stack beneath the lower left of the game board. Depending on your player count, draw a number of cattle cards from this stack:
second-edition.further-steps-step4_simmental: Add all 8 stage 1 "Simmental" cattle cards to the 36 market cattle cards, shuffle them and place them as a face-down market cattle stack beneath the lower left of the game board. Place the Simmental market tile on the game board so that it covers the corresponding area of the cattle market. Then, depending on your player count, draw a number of cattle cards from this stack:
second-edition.further-steps-step4_brahman: Add all 8 "Brahman" cattle cards to the 36 market cattle cards, shuffle them and place them as a face-down market cattle stack beneath the lower left of the game board. Place the Simmental market tile on the game board so that it covers the corresponding area of the cattle market.
second-edition.further-steps-simmental2: Progress as usual but place the orange cattle cards to the very left (if there are any).
second-edition.further-steps-simmental3: Take the stage 2 and stage 3 cards and place them in separate stacks next to the game board (close to Kansas City)
second-edition.further-steps-brahman: Progress as usual but place the orange cattle cards to the very left (if there are any).
second-edition.further-steps-step5: Shuffle the 24 objective cards and place them as a face-down stack to the right of the game board. Then reveal 4 cards from this stack and display them face up in a column below the stack
second-edition.further-steps-step61: 1st player: 6 dollars, 4 cards and 1 exchange token
second-edition.further-steps-step62: 2nd player: 7 dollars, 5 cards and 1 exchange token
second-edition.further-steps-step63: 3rd player: 8 dollars, 6 cards and 1 exchange token
second-edition.further-steps-step64: 4th player: 9 dollars, 7 cards and 1 exchange token
second-edition.further-steps-step7: Place the extension board above the base game board and slide it over the top edge, covering the original city strip with the new city strip.
second-edition.further-steps-step8: Shuffle the 10 medium town tiles face down. Then draw 6 of these tiles and place one at random face up onto each of the 6 medium town spaces on the extension board. Return the 4 spare tiles to the box.
second-edition.further-steps-step9: Add the 2 new station master tiles to the ones from the base game and shuffle these 11 tiles.
second-edition.further-steps-step91: Place one at random face up onto each of the 5 station master spaces of the base game board.
second-edition.further-steps-step92: Then place one at random face up onto each of the 2 station master spaces of the extension board.
second-edition.further-steps-step93: Return the remaining 4 tiles to the box.
second-edition.further-steps-step10: Receive the following items of your color:
second-edition.further-steps-step101: The new private building tile, which you add to your other building tiles. (As always, all players must have the same sides of their building tiles face up.)
second-edition.further-steps-step102: The auxiliary add-on, which you place to the left of your player board.
second-edition.further-steps-step103: The additional player dise, which you place onto the colored disc space of your auxiliary add-on.
second-edition.further-steps-step104: The 15 branchlets, which you place in groups of 3 onto each of the 5 rows of your auxiliary add-on.
second-edition.further-steps-step11: Add the 4 new objective cards with the expansion icon * to the 24 objective cards of the base game. Shuffle those 28 objective cards and place them as a face-down stack to the right of the game board. Then reveal 4 cards from this stack and display them face up in a column below the stack. This display is called the genereal objective display.
second-edition.further-steps-step11_1: Add the 1 new starting objective card with the expansion icon * to the 4 starting objective cards of the base game. Shuffle those 5 starting objective cards and deal each player one random card, which you place face up below your player board. This is the first card of your personal objective area.
second-edition.further-steps-step11_2: Finally, return any remaining starting objective cards to the box.
second-edition.further-steps-step11_3: If you are playing the Simmental or Brahman variant you may further add the 4 new objective cards and the 1 new starting objective card with the orange cattle icon the same way.

variant-warning.title: Editor's note
variant-warning.content: Even though it is possible to add both the "Brahman" and the "Simmental" cattle cards, we do not recommend this because it completely changes the balance of the market cattle stack. That's why we decide to use the same color for the 2 different types of cattle cards.

new-zealand.app-title: Great Western Trail New Zealand Setup Randomizer
new-zealand.further-steps-step1: From the Wellington supply, first pick 1 of each worker type from the "A"-bag and place them onto the indicated spaces of the top row in the job market:
new-zealand.further-steps-step2: Then, depending on your player count, draw a number of tiles from the "A"-bag:
new-zealand.further-steps-step3: Start drawing bonus tiles from the "B"-bag. Put these bonus tiles one after the other in the bonus tiles market until you reach the market token
new-zealand.further-steps-step4: Now fill the 4 foresight spaces in Wellington with tiles from the Wellington supply
new-zealand.further-steps-step5: Shuffle the 37 market sheep cards and place them as a face-down market stack beneath the lower right of the game board. Depending on your player count, draw a number of sheep cards from this stack:
new-zealand.further-steps-step6: Shuffle the 24 objective cards and place them as a face-down stack to the right of the game board. Then reveal 4 cards from this stack and display them face-up in a column below the stack.
new-zealand.further-steps-step7: Place the 18 Romney sheep cards, the 12 Sheepdog cards, the 14 Ferry cards, and the 12 Kōtare bird cards on the left side of the game board
new-zealand.further-steps-step8: Add a number of cards to the randomly picked bonus card sets depending on the player count:
new-zealand.further-steps-step81: In a 2-player game: Add 2 cards for a total of 3
new-zealand.further-steps-step82: In a 3-player game: Add 4 cards for a total of 5
new-zealand.further-steps-step83: In a 4-player game: Add all 5 cards for a total of 6
new-zealand.further-steps-step9: Then place the 4 bonus cards tiles next to the sea routes board and the stack with
new-zealand.further-steps-step91: the lowest set number (out of the 4 determined sets) below the tile with the steering wheel
new-zealand.further-steps-step92: the second lowest set number goes below the tile with the barrel
new-zealand.further-steps-step93: the stack with the second highest set number below the bell
new-zealand.further-steps-step94: the highest set number below the bonus card tile with the compass
new-zealand.further-steps-step10: Place a number of step tiles equal to your player count onto the respective orange space next to the pathfinder track.
new-zealand.further-steps-step111: 1st player: 7 pounds, 4 cards and 1 exchange token
new-zealand.further-steps-step112: 2nd player: 8 pounds, 5 cards and 1 exchange token
new-zealand.further-steps-step113: 3rd player: 9 pounds, 6 cards and 1 exchange token
new-zealand.further-steps-step114: 4th player: 10 pounds, 7 cards and 1 exchange token
